event: The Judgment of Solomon — deciding which woman is the true mother
symbols: sword raised over the infant — the test of true motherhood through the threat of division
Solomon judging — divine wisdom granted by God for righteous governance
one mother pleading — sacrificial love willing to give up her claim to save the child
the disputed infant — the innocent at the center of human conflict
description: A fresco showing a dramatic scene with several figures. A muscular man raises a sword over an infant, preparing to cut the child in two. A woman reaches out in distress to stop him. Another figure, likely Solomon, is seated or standing in a position of authority. The composition is tight and dynamic, rendered in warm earth tones with strong figure modeling characteristic of Renaissance fresco. The scene is framed within a decorative border.
theological_description: The Judgment of Solomon (1 Kings 3:16-28) is the paradigmatic biblical narrative of divinely granted wisdom applied to human governance. When two women both claimed to be the mother of a living infant, Solomon ordered the child cut in two — knowing that the true mother would rather give up her claim than see her child killed. The real mother cried out: 'Please, my lord, give her the living baby! Don't kill him!' while the false mother said, 'Neither I nor you shall have him. Cut him in two!' (1 Kings 3:26). Solomon's brilliance lay not in Solomonic compromise but in understanding that sacrificial love reveals truth. This wisdom was God's direct gift in answer to Solomon's prayer: 'Give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to distinguish between right and wrong' (1 Kings 3:9). The people's response — 'they held the king in awe, because they saw that he had wisdom from God to administer justice' (1 Kings 3:28) — established Solomon as the archetype of the wise ruler. Christ pointed beyond Solomon: 'one greater than Solomon is here' (Matthew 12:42), declaring Himself the ultimate source of divine wisdom.

scripture_refs.reason: The Judgment of Solomon — the direct narrative of the two mothers and the disputed infant
Solomon's prayer for a discerning heart — the divine wisdom underlying his judgment
All Israel holding the king in awe for his God-given wisdom
One greater than Solomon is here — Christ as the ultimate fulfillment of divine wisdom
